examples.input: Which of the following compounds does not undergo the iodoform reaction?
Which of the following inorganic acids can dissolve $SiO_2$?
Which of the following groups consists entirely of amphoteric substances?
In photography, $Na_2S_2O_3$ is used to wash away unexposed $AgBr$, and then $Na_2S$ is used to recover the dissolved silver. The two types of reactions involved are, respectively, ______.

In a reaction equation, if the absolute values of the stoichiometric coefficients of the reactants are exactly equal to the exponents of the concentrations of the reactants in the rate equation, then the reaction ____.
When a ground-state atom has only two electrons in its sixth electron shell, the number of electrons in its second-to-outermost shell is ______.

An alkali mixture is titrated with a standard HCl solution using phenolphthalein as the indicator, consuming $V_1$ mL of the HCl solution to reach the endpoint. Subsequently, the solution is titrated using methyl orange as the indicator to the endpoint, consuming an additional $V_2$ mL of the HCl solution. If $V_2 > V_1$, the composition of the sample is ( ).
When acid is added to an aqueous solution of $[FeF_6]^{3-}$, which of the following statements is correct?
Given that the unit of the rate constant $k$ for a certain reaction is $L \cdot mol^{-1} \cdot s^{-1}$, the order of the reaction is ______.

At 298 K and under constant pressure, the standard molar enthalpies of reaction $\Delta_rH_m^\ominus$ for 1 mol of white phosphorus and 1 mol of red phosphorus reacting with excess $Cl_2(g)$ to form $PCl_5(s)$ are $-447.1\, \text{kJ}\cdot\text{mol}^{-1}$ and $-429.5\, \text{kJ}\cdot\text{mol}^{-1}$, respectively. The standard molar enthalpies of formation $\Delta_fH_m^\ominus$ (298 K) of white phosphorus and red phosphorus are:
Consider the following group of compounds: acetone, cyclohexanone, benzophenone, and formaldehyde. Rank the compounds in order of decreasing reaction rate in the nucleophilic addition of $HCN$.
When water vaporizes at a given temperature and saturation vapor pressure, which of the following changes in state functions is zero?
Which of the following is not a reducing sugar?
In a buffer solution at pH = 6, what is the predominant form of lysine (pI = 9.74)?

The $Ca^{2+}$ ion is precipitated as $CaC_2O_4$, which is then dissolved in acid. The resulting $H_2C_2O_4$ is titrated with a standard $KMnO_4$ solution to determine the calcium content. Which titration method is used?
Which of the following compounds exhibits the highest reaction rate when reacting with an aqueous solution of $KOH$ via the $S_N2$ mechanism?
In quantitative analysis, the purpose of performing replicate measurements on the same sample is to ____.
Which of the following statements correctly describes the solubility of an amino acid in water and the concentration of zwitterions at the isoelectric point?
In which of the following molecules is the central atom sp-hybridized?
Which of the following compounds has the highest pKa?
Assuming equal molality, which of the following aqueous solutions has the highest boiling point?
Under identical conditions, the freezing point of aqueous solution A is higher than that of aqueous solution B. How do the boiling points of the two aqueous solutions compare?

At 298 K and 100 kPa, the activation energy of an elementary reaction is $10.7\, \text{kJ} \cdot \text{mol}^{-1}$ and the enthalpy change of the reaction is $-193.8\, \text{kJ} \cdot \text{mol}^{-1}$. The activation energy of the reverse reaction is ( )
At room temperature, which of the following molecules has a non-zero dipole moment?
When an ammonia solution is diluted with three times its volume of water, the pH of the solution will ______.
At equal concentration, which of the following hydrohalic acids is the strongest?
Which of the following methods is the most suitable for separating cinnamyl alcohol (bp ~257°C) and cinnamaldehyde (bp ~253°C)?
The freezing point of an aqueous solution is measured to be -0.186°C. Which of the following statements is correct? ($K_f=1.86\ ^\circ\text{C}/m$, $K_b=0.512\ ^\circ\text{C}/m$)
Given that a 5% w/w glucose ($C_6H_{12}O_6$) solution is isotonic to blood, how does the osmotic pressure of a 5% w/w urea $[(NH_2)_2CO]$ solution compare to that of blood?
During the complexometric titration of  metal ions with EDTA, if the acidity of the medium is not controlled by a buffer solution, how does the pH change in the system?
Which of the following complexes has a stability least affected by acidity?
Which of the following compounds does not react with Tollens' reagent?
The boiling point of $H_2O$ is 100°C, while that of $H_2Se$ is -42°C. Which of the following best explains the difference?
Which of the following compounds has the strongest basicity?
In the determination of ammonia concentration by back titration, a blank titration is usually required. Which of the following statements regarding the back titration is incorrect?
Which of the following groups contains the glycosidic bond types that are found in starch molecules?

When 0.45 g of a non-electrolyte is dissolved in 30 g of water, the resulting solution has a freezing point of -0.15°C. Given that the $K_f$ of water is $1.86\, \mathrm{K \cdot kg \cdot mol^{-1}}$, the molar mass (g·mol⁻¹) of the non-electrolyte is approximately ______.

Dilute hydrochloric acid is added to an aqueous mixture of glutamic acid ($pI=3.22$), valine ($pI=5.96$), and arginine ($pI=10.76$) until the pH of the solution reaches 6. Which of the following amino acids will exist predominantly in the cationic form?
In the presence of peroxides, the reaction of alkenes with HBr proceeds via which of the following mechanisms?
What is the precipitate produced when an aqueous solution of $Pb(NO_3)_2$ is added to an aqueous solution of $K_2Cr_2O_7$?
If 0.15 L of oxygen at $9.8 \times 10^4$ Pa and 0.30 L of nitrogen at $4.66 \times 10^5$ Pa are introduced into a 0.30 L evacuated container, the total pressure of the gas mixture is ______ Pa.
Given the order of acidity: water > ethanol > acetylene, which of the following statements is incorrect?
How many isomers are possible for the complex ion $[Co(NH_3)_4(H_2O)_2]^{3+}$?
Given the reaction $A \rightleftharpoons B$, where $k_{fwd} = 100 k_{rev}$ at 298 K, what is the relationship between the activation energies of the forward and reverse reactions?
In which of the following reactions will the rate decrease as the reagent concentration decreases?
Which of the following does not undergo hydrolysis in a dilute acid solution?

At constant pressure, $NaCl$ crystals, sucrose crystals, and their saturated aqueous solution coexist in equilibrium. Determine the number of independent components $C$ and the degrees of freedom $f^{\prime\prime}$:

Two tiny mercury droplets of unequal size are present in a sealed container at a constant temperature. After a sufficiently long time, liquid mercury still exists in the container. Which of the following statements is correct?
In which direction does the excess pressure act in a water droplet and an air bubble, respectively?
A clean glass capillary tube is placed horizontally and contains a column of water that can move freely. Which of the following will occur if the right end of the water column is gently heated?
When a highly concentrated electrolyte solution is diluted at a constant temperature, how will the conductivity $\kappa$ and molar conductivity $\Lambda_m$ change?
Salt is used on icy roads in winter to prevent slipping. Which property of dilute solutions is involved?
Given that osmosis does not occur between two dilute electrolyte solutions, which of the following statements is correct?

To determine the CaO content in limestone using an EDTA standard solution with $c(H_2Y^{2-}) = 0.02\, \text{mol} \cdot \text{L}^{-1}$, assume the sample contains approximately 50% CaO. The sample is dissolved and diluted to 250.00 mL, from which a 25.00 mL aliquot is taken for titration. To control the measurement error to within a few parts per thousand, what is an appropriate range for the sample mass? (Given: relative molecular mass of CaO is 56.08)
When a small amount of $AgNO_3$ is added to a $[FeCl(H_2O)_5]Br$ solution, what is the main component of the precipitate produced?
Which of the following can undergo a coupling reaction with benzenediazonium chloride to form an azo compound under weakly alkaline conditions?

4.5 g of a non-electrolyte is dissolved in 125 g of water, resulting in a solution with a freezing point of -0.372°C. What is the relative molecular mass of the substance? ($K_f = 1.86 \text{°C} \cdot \text{kg} \cdot \text{mol}^{-1}$)
An $AgCl$ sol is prepared by mixing a slight excess of potassium chloride solution with silver nitrate solution. Which of the following electrolytes has the strongest coagulating power?
In which of the following pairs of molecules do the central atoms exhibit non-equivalent sp^3 hybridization?
At 25°C, the solubility products of $AgCl$ and $Ag_2CrO_4$ are $1.56\times 10^{-10}$ and $1.12\times 10^{-12}$, respectively. How does the solubility of $AgCl$ compare to that of $Ag_2CrO_4$?
Which of the following acts as a meta-director on a benzene ring?

During the standardization of HCl with borax, a student stirred the solution in the Erlenmeyer flask with a glass rod while dissolving the borax, and then removed the rod without rinsing it. As a result, the standardized concentration will be ___.
Which of the following is a heterocyclic compound?

Let $S_0$, $S_1$, $S_2$, and $S_3$ be the solubilities of $AgCl(s)$ in water, in a $CaCl_2$ solution with $c(CaCl_2)=0.01\, \text{mol} \cdot \text{L}^{-1}$, in a $NaCl$ solution with $c(NaCl)=0.01\, \text{mol} \cdot \text{L}^{-1}$, and in a $AgNO_3$ solution with $c(AgNO_3)=0.05\, \text{mol} \cdot \text{L}^{-1}$, respectively. Which of the following relationships is correct?
In which of the following media is mutarotation observed for methyl-α-D-glucopyranoside?
In which of the following scenarios is the hydrolysis of haloalkanes NOT an $S_N2$ reaction?
Let the four quantum numbers n, l, m, and m_s represent the state of an electron. Which of the following configurations is invalid?
Which of the following fatty acids is unsaturated?

Rank the following compounds in order of decreasing basicity:
① Benzamide ② Phthalimide ③ Urea ④ Tetramethylammonium hydroxide
Which of the following carbonyl compounds has the highest relative reactivity toward nucleophilic addition?
Given that an amino acid dissolves completely in pure water at pH 7, and the resulting solution has a pH of 6, the isoelectric point of this amino acid is:
When an amino acid is dissolved in water at pH 8.5 and placed in an electric field, it moves toward the cathode. This indicates that:
Which of the following compounds causes protein precipitation and denaturation?
Which of the following compounds has the highest melting point?

Two equal-volume aliquots of a mixed solution contain equimolar amounts of $NaOH$ and $Na_2CO_3$. One is titrated using phenolphthalein as the indicator, and the volume of HCl consumed to reach the endpoint is $V_1$; the other is titrated using methyl orange as the indicator, and the volume of HCl of the same concentration consumed to reach the endpoint is $V_2$. The relationship between $V_1$ and $V_2$ is ______.
Which of the following aqueous solutions, each 5% by mass, has the lowest normal boiling point?
Which of the following compounds yields a different osazone from the others when reacted with phenylhydrazine?
In an aqueous solution at pH 6.0, which of the following amino acids migrates toward the cathode when placed in an electric field?
Which of the following compounds produces turbidity when treated with Lucas reagent at room temperature?
To dissolve $Mg(OH)_2$, one can add:
Which of the following compounds has the highest boiling point?
A system absorbs 100 kJ of heat from its surroundings and does 25 kJ of work on its surroundings during a process. What is the internal energy change of the system?
Which of the following groups exerts a greater +C effect than its -I effect when attached to a benzene ring?
Which of the following compounds forms a cyclic anhydride upon heating?
Propanal is treated with ethylmagnesium bromide, followed by hydrolysis. What is the major product?
For a system that undergoes a series of changes and returns to its initial state, which of the following relationships must hold?
In an electrophilic aromatic substitution, which of the following is a meta-director?

Consider two crystal forms of elemental sulfur: monoclinic and orthorhombic. At standard pressure, orthorhombic sulfur is more stable at temperatures below 21.36 K. Conversely, monoclinic sulfur is more stable at temperatures above this point. Therefore, the reaction $S(monoclinic) \rightarrow S(orthorhombic)$ is ______.
Crops in saline soil grow poorly or even wither. Which of the following properties of dilute solutions best explains this?

When 13.0 g of a non-electrolyte is dissolved in 200 g of water, the solution has a freezing point of -1.95°C. Given that $K_f = 1.86\, \text{°C} \cdot \text{kg} \cdot \text{mol}^{-1} $, determine the molar mass (M) of the substance.
Which of the following compounds produces turbidity the most rapidly when reacting with Lucas reagent at room temperature?

A galvanic cell is based on the reaction $Fe(s)+2Ag^{+}(aq) \rightarrow Fe^{2+}(aq)+2Ag(s)$. If the concentration of $Ag^+$ is reduced to one-tenth of its original concentration, determine the change in the electromotive force.
The development and use of new energy are crucial for the sustainable development of human society. Which of the following is a new energy source?
Which of the following experimental observations is incorrect?
Which of the following ancient Chinese inventions or technologies primarily involves a chemical change?
Which of the following changes is not an oxidation reaction?
From a safety perspective, which of the following practices is correct?
Which of the following reagents yields three distinct observations when added to solutions of $Ca(OH)_2$, $H_2SO_4$, and $Na_2SO_4$?
Chemistry is closely associated with industry and daily life. Which of the following statements is correct?
Which of the following correctly identifies the number of isomers (excluding stereoisomers) for the organic compounds represented by the molecular formulas?

Organic compound A ($C_7H_{12}O_2$) contains a six-membered ring. Under acidic conditions, it can undergo hydrolysis, yielding a single product. Additionally, the molecule of A contains two methyl groups. How many possible structures does A have?
Excluding stereoisomers, how many isomers with the molecular formula $C_4H_8O$ contain a three-membered ring?
How many aromatic isomers exist with the molecular formula $C_7H_6O_2$?

When metallic sodium dissolves in liquid ammonia, it forms ammoniated sodium ions and ammoniated electrons. Adding the cryptand ligand L to this solution yields the first compound containing an alkali metal anion, [$NaL^{+}Na^{-}], which appears golden-yellow. Which of the following statements is incorrect?
An ideal atom-economical reaction is one in which all atoms of the reactants are converted into the desired final products. Which of the following is considered an ideal atom-economical reaction?
Chemistry is closely related to industry and daily life. Which of the following statements is correct?

A solution may contain $Cl^-$, $SO_4^{2-}$, $CO_3^{2-}$, $NH_4^+$, $Fe^{3+}$, $Al^{3+}$, and $K^+$. A 100 mL sample of this solution was treated with an excess of $NaOH$ solution and heated. This process yielded 0.02 mol of gas and a reddish-brown precipitate, which was then filtered, washed, and ignited to obtain 1.6 g of solid. A sufficient amount of $BaCl_2$ solution was added to the filtrate, producing 4.66 g of a precipitate insoluble in hydrochloric acid. From the above observations, it can be concluded that in the original solution:

A solution may contain only $Na^+$, $NH_4^+$, $Ba^{2+}$, $SO_4^{2-}$, $I^-$, and $S^{2-}$. The following tests are conducted on separate portions: ① measurement with a pH meter, indicating the solution is weakly acidic; ② addition of chlorine water and starch, producing no observable change. Which of the following ions still requires testing before the composition can be determined?
Which of the following statements concerning the two gases $^{12}C^{18}O$ and $^{14}N_2$ at the same temperature and pressure is correct?

A small beaker containing $NH_4HCO_3$ powder is placed into a large beaker containing a small amount of acetic acid. Hydrochloric acid is then added to the small beaker. The reaction proceeds vigorously, and the acetic acid gradually solidifies. Which of the following conclusions is correct?
Given the thermochemical equation $CO(g) + H_2O(g) \rightarrow H_2(g) + CO_2(g)$ $\Delta H < 0$, which of the following statements is correct, with all other conditions held constant?

$(NH_4)_2SO_4$ decomposes into $SO_2$, $N_2$, $NH_3$, and $H_2O$ upon strong heating. In this reaction, what is the ratio of the number of nitrogen atoms that undergo a change in oxidation state to the number of those that do not?
When 0.195 g of zinc powder is added to 20.0 mL of 0.100 mol/L $MO_2^+$ solution, the reaction proceeds exactly to completion. Which of the following is a possible reduction product?

Lithium imide ($Li_2NH$) is a solid hydrogen storage material characterized by high storage capacity and safety. Its hydrogen storage mechanism can be represented as $Li_2NH + H_2 \rightleftharpoons LiNH_2 + LiH$. Which of the following statements is correct?

Zinc and dilute nitric acid react in a molar ratio of 2:5. During the reaction, the nitric acid is reduced to $N_2O$, and the zinc is completely consumed upon completion of the reaction. What is the molar ratio of the reduced nitric acid to the unreduced nitric acid?

X, Y, Z and W are elements from the first three periods, arranged in order of increasing atomic numbers. The sum of the outermost electrons in their atoms is 13. The atomic radius of X is smaller than that of Y. X and W belong to the same main group. Z is the most abundant element in the Earth's crust. Which of the following statements is correct?

W, X, Y, and Z are main-group elements from the first three periods, arranged in order of increasing atomic number. The ratio of the number of electrons in the outermost shells of atoms W to X is 4:3, and Z has 4 more electrons than X in total. Which of the following statements is correct?
Which of the following changes requires overcoming the same type of forces?

X, Y, Z, and W are elements from the first three periods. X does not possess a stable octet in its valence shell, and its elemental form is obtained industrially through the fractional distillation of liquid air. Y has an equal number of s and p electrons in its valence shell. The electron configuration of the +2 cation of element Z is identical to that of a neon atom. W has one unpaired p electron in its M shell. Which of the following statements regarding the properties of these elements is necessarily true?
W, X, Y and Z are elements from the first three periods, arranged in order of increasing atomic number. Which of the following pairs of simple ions both disturb the water ionization equilibrium?
Given that $_{33}As$ and $_{35}Br$ are in the same period, which of the following comparisons is correct?

W, X, Y, and Z are elements from the first three periods, arranged in order of increasing atomic number. The number of electrons in the anion of W is identical to that of the inner-shell electrons in atoms X, Y, and Z. A nuclide of X is commonly used to date artifacts in archaeology. Elemental Y is produced industrially by the fractional distillation of liquid air. Z cannot form diatomic molecules. Based on the above description, which of the following statements is correct?
If the reaction $X(g) + Y(g) \rightarrow 2Z(g)$ ($\Delta H < 0$) is at equilibrium, which of the following statements is correct?
For a $0.10 \text{ mol}\cdot\text{L}^{-1}$ aqueous ammonia solution at room temperature, which of the following is correct?

Supercritical water (above 374°C and 22.1 MPa) exhibits a strong ability to dissolve organic compounds and contains a high concentration of $H^+$ and $OH^-$. Which of the following can be inferred from the description above?
Kansas is rich in rock salt (composed primarily of $NaCl$, along with other soluble impurities such as sulfate ions $SO_4^{2-}$). Which of the following statements is correct?

A common deoxidizer in pastry packaging contains reduced iron powder, sodium chloride, carbon powder, etc., and the deoxidation mechanism is identical to the oxygen absorption corrosion of steel. Which of the following statements is correct?
Which of the following measures for preventing metal corrosion uses impressed current cathodic protection (ICCP)?
Which of the following states related to the oxygen atom requires the least energy to remove an electron?
Aria is conducting an experiment using a candle. Which of the following statements is incorrect?
Which of the following substances produces a shower of sparks when burning in oxygen?
Which of the following groups of substances is arranged in the order of simple substance, compound, and mixture?
Which of the following statements regarding sucrose (chemical formula $C_{12}H_{22}O_{11}$) is true?
Burning cigarettes produce smoke that contains nicotine (chemical formula $\text{C}_{10}\text{H}_{14}\text{N}_2$). Which of the following statements is correct?
A pure substance completely combusts in oxygen, producing only carbon dioxide and water. Which of the following can be inferred about the pure substance?
Which of the following statements is incorrect?
Which of the following statements regarding carbon and carbon oxides is correct?
Which of the following statements regarding chemical experiments is incorrect?
A student is preparing 50 g of a 15% (w/w) sodium chloride solution. Which of the following statements is correct?
Which of the following statements regarding solutions is correct?
Which of the following devices are NOT used in the laboratory preparation of oxygen using potassium permanganate?
A correct sequence of steps is critical to success in experiments. Regarding what should be done **first** and **next**, which of the following statements is incorrect?
Adam notices that the crops in his field have yellowing leaves and weak stems that are prone to lodging. Which of the following fertilizers should he apply?
Which of the following cannot be used to distinguish between sodium hydroxide solution and dilute hydrochloric acid?
Which of the following describes a type of change fundamentally different from the others?
Chlorosulfonic acid ($ClSO_3H$) is a strong acid. Given the reaction $ClSO_3H + HCOOH \rightarrow CO + HCl + H_2SO_4$, which of the following statements is correct?

A certain amount of charcoal is burned in a sealed container with a mixture of nitrogen and oxygen, producing $CO$ and $CO_2$. The mass fraction of carbon in the resulting gas mixture of $CO$, $CO_2$, and $N_2$ is measured to be 24%. What is a possible value of the mass fraction of nitrogen in the mixture?

A beaker is filled with an acetic acid solution of a certain concentration (density approximately 1 g/cm³). After the beaker is emptied, 0.5 mL of the solution remains. The beaker is then rinsed twice, each time with 20 mL of distilled water. What fraction of the original concentration is that of the residual acetic acid solution in the beaker after the rinsing?

When an egg is placed in white vinegar, bubbles form on the surface of the shell. Is the gas produced carbon dioxide? Steven suggested using limewater to test this. Which of the following parts of scientific inquiry best describes this step?

A student used a graduated cylinder to measure the volume of a liquid. The cylinder was placed on a level surface with the graduations facing the student. They first took a reading of 19 mL by viewing the bottom of the meniscus at eye level. After pouring out some of the liquid, they took a reading of 10 mL by viewing the scale from above. The actual volume of the liquid poured out is ___.

A student used a graduated cylinder to measure liquid. The cylinder was placed on a level surface. With the bottom of the meniscus at eye level, the student first recorded 20 mL. After pouring out some of the liquid, they took a reading of 15 mL by viewing the meniscus from below. Which statement about the actual volume of liquid poured out is correct?
Which of the following involves a chemical change?
Which of the following applications relies on the chemical properties of the substance?
In which of the following pairs of changes is the first a chemical change and the second a physical change?
Which of the following kitchen items is primarily made of synthetic material?
Which of the materials used in the following items is NOT a synthetic organic polymer?

Insufficient zinc intake can lead to various health issues. Under medical supervision, individuals with zinc deficiency can take an oral solution of zinc gluconate ($C_{12}H_{22}O_{14}Zn$) as a supplement. Which of the following statements about zinc gluconate is incorrect?
